paragraphs.text: DAVID: ABBA FATHER, what does meekness of love through persecution mean?

JESUS: In order to reach the top of the Holy Mountain, meekness or gentleness is another requirement that will make one great in holiness so as to reach the Holy Mountain to inherit the Kingdom of Heaven.
Psa 18:35 Thou hast also given me the shield of thy salvation: and thy right hand hath holden me up, and thy gentleness (meekness) hath made me great.
I take delight in the meek and will beautify them with the robe of salvation as My heavenly bride.
Psa 149:4 For THE YAHWEH taketh pleasure in his people: he will beautify the meek with salvation.
I give grace to the meek so that they can inherit My glory and the earth.
Pro 3:34 Surely he scorneth the scorners: but he giveth grace unto the meek. Pro 3:35 The wise shall inherit glory: but shame shall be the promotion of fools.
Mat 5:5 Blessed are the meek (G4239 praus): for they shall inherit the earth.
Those who follow Me to be meek and lowly in heart shall enter into Our rest in Heavenly Mount Zion.

Mat 11:28 Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest. Mat 11:29 Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me; for I am meek and lowly in heart: and ye shall find rest unto your souls. Mat 11:30 For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.

When a person is so broken and crushed by My FATHER through persecution, the meekness of a Christ/an anointed will be evident in him. His spirit will release a sweet smell like the fragrant of perfume that is obtained from crushed myrrh when mixed with olive oil. Myrrh symbolizes suffering and oil symbolizes the anointing. This fruit of meekness, like other fruit of the spirit, needs to be maintained when yielded. We have appointed at least 15 years to break and crush you in order to accomplish this state of spirit man on its sustainable basis. Pruning is painful but it is necessary if you are to produce more fruits of the Spirit. Each time when my FATHER withdrew His grace from you, hell will break loose. According to what fruit He wanted you to bear, He would show you the worst in you concerning that fruit you lack. Sometimes, you will fall into condemnation by the Accuser and felt very discouraged and nearly gave up but abiding in My FATHER'S love will keep you going.

Joh 15:1 I am the true vine, and my Father is the husbandman. Joh 15:2 Every branch in me that beareth not fruit he taketh away: and every branch that beareth fruit, he purgeth it, that it may bring forth more fruit. Joh 15:3 Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you. Joh 15:4 Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, except ye abide in me. Joh 15:5 I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing. Joh 15:6 If a man abide not in me, he is cast forth as a branch, and is withered; and men gather them, and cast them into the fire, and they are burned. Joh 15:7 If ye abide in me and my speech (G 4487 rhema") abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you. Joh 15:8 Herein is my Father glorified, that ye bear much fruit; so shall ye be my disciples. Joh 15:9 As THE FATHER hath loved me, so have I loved you: continue ye in my love. Joh 15:10 If ye keep my commandments, ye shall abide in my love; even as I have kept my Father's commandments, and abide in his love. Joh 15:11 These things have I spoken unto you, that my joy might remain in you, and that your joy might be full. Joh 15:12 This is my commandment, That ye love one another, as I have loved you. Joh 15:13 Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends. Joh 15:14 Ye are my friends, if ye do whatsoever I command you. Joh 15:15 Henceforth I call you not servants; for the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth: but I have called you friends; for all things that I have heard of my Father I have made known unto you. Joh 15:16 Ye have not chosen me, but I have chosen you, and ordained you, that ye should go and bring forth fruit, and that your fruit should remain: that whatsoever ye shall ask of THE FATHER in my name, he may give it you.

David, would you like to share with SHEKINAIH how it is like to be meek? DAVID: When I am broken, I would speak in a soft, feeble and humble tone; but when I have recovered from this brokenness and was not vigilant, the

spirit of pride would sneak back into me and my speech would become arrogant and harsh. Maybe I need further breaking until it becomes sustainable where I will remain gentle and meek, loving my persecutors. To be crushed by THE FATHER is not easy. It is very hard to go through and at times, I will break down and cry like a baby. Have you ever seen an adult man who cries like a baby during the breaking? This is what THE FATHER has planned for every one of us.
